title: Five Questions On China Biotech
text:
Five Questions On China Biotech

Tom Hancock, AJ Cortese, Huang Shichan

Gavekal Technologies: Briefing | 13 Jul 2026

Investors have cooled on China’s biotechnology sector, after a spectacular run-up in Hong Kong biotech stocks in early 2025. This is a needed correction: China’s biotech companies are technologically strong, but commercially immature. They are on track to sign US$200bn in cross-border licensing deals this year, and can develop new drugs far faster than foreign rivals, thanks to a strong domestic ecosystem boasting abundant talent, streamlined regulation and effective research networks. But a small domestic market and cutthroat competition make them heavily reliant on overseas licensing deals in which their big pharma licensees reap most of the profit. Today’s note tackles the five biggest questions facing the sector.Also in today’s China Tech Brief:
